target_semantics: Start in normal state and support a reliable normal-to-insert-to-normal editing roundtrip.
divergence: none
notes: Scope is the tested startup and insert roundtrip, not every Evil command.

target_semantics: Use Space as leader in normal and visual states and resolve every declared Lem leader chord to its intended command.
divergence: none
notes: Command-level parity is tracked separately where implementations diverge.

target_semantics: Send the current region or buffer to the LLM from insert state with C-c i.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Binding exists but no end-to-end network-backed test exists.

target_semantics: C-u in insert state deletes back to indentation without leaving insert state.
divergence: none

target_semantics: C-n and C-p remain ordinary logical-line movement outside completion-specific keymaps.
divergence: none
notes: Emacs achieves this by unbinding Evil overrides; Lem binds the intended fall-through behavior directly.

target_semantics: Provide the configured global window, duplicate, recent-file, grep, find-name, and buffer-list bindings.
divergence: Recent-file and find-name bindings are absent; duplicate is line-only rather than duplicate-dwim.
notes: M-o, M-j, M-s g, and C-x C-b have Lem counterparts.

target_semantics: Provide mode-local Claude Code, Notmuch payment-email, Elfeed archive, Salta, and project outline bindings.
divergence: No project-local consult-outline binding and Notmuch payment-email bridge is not reproduced.
notes: Individual app behavior remains separately tracked.

target_semantics: Support ys, ds, cs, visual S, and padded delimiter surround editing while retaining native operators.
divergence: Tag prompts and syntax-aware balancing are missing.
notes: Core tested grammar works, but the known missing grammar prevents an exact claim.

target_semantics: Provide visible-scope two-character snipe motions, repeat, and operator integration.
divergence: Incremental highlighting is absent and f/t are not replaced by snipe.
notes: s and S plus repeat and inclusive operator behavior are tested.

target_semantics: Use gc as a normal-state operator and visual-state action with language-appropriate line comments.
divergence: none
notes: Verified in Lisp and Python buffers.

target_semantics: Repeated SPC v expands the selection through useful syntactic units.
divergence: Expansion is delimiter and line based rather than parser-backed expreg semantics.
notes: The tested word-to-enclosing-pair progression is preserved.

target_semantics: Enable delimiter-safe Lispyville-style operators, atom movement, slurp/barf, and configured structural transforms in all configured Lisp-family buffers.
divergence: none
notes: Exact is limited to the configured key theme and the behavior matrix exercised by the 20 TUI checks.

target_semantics: Jump to visible lines, characters, and symbols with Avy-style target selection.
divergence: Lem uses goto-line, snipe, and symbol isearch rather than on-screen target hints.
notes: Bindings preserve destinations but not Avy interaction semantics.

target_semantics: Show distinct terminal cursor colors or shapes for insert, normal, visual, replace, and Emacs states.
divergence: No state-specific cursor appearance configuration is present.
notes: The normal-state modeline indicator is covered separately.

target_semantics: Retain Vim linewise operators, counts, dot-repeat, and register semantics alongside custom operator dispatch.
divergence: none
notes: Scope is the explicitly tested operator and register behavior.

target_semantics: Use spaces for indentation with tab width four and allow mode-specific indentation rules.
divergence: Tab width is configured, but a global no-tabs policy and editorconfig integration are not established.

target_semantics: Trim trailing whitespace only on lines touched during editing when saving a programming buffer.
divergence: none
notes: The hermetic Lem test covers insertion and deletion tracking, untouched dirty whitespace, tracking reset, clean repeated saves, and prose exclusion.

target_semantics: Automatically insert balanced delimiter pairs globally.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Paredit pairing is tested for Lisp, but global electric-pair behavior has not been assessed.

target_semantics: Typing replaces an active selection.
divergence: unassessed
notes: No behavior test or source audit records Lem selection replacement semantics.

target_semantics: Provide built-in undo and redo with raised history limits plus a navigable visual undo tree.
divergence: Linear undo and redo exist, but vundo-style tree visualization and matching limits are absent.

target_semantics: M-j duplicates the active region when present, otherwise the current line.
divergence: The Lem command duplicates only the current line.

target_semantics: Fill the current paragraph and optionally auto-fill while typing at the configured column.
divergence: none
notes: Exact is limited to the tested plain-text paragraph and buffer-local toggle semantics.

target_semantics: Disable lockfiles, backup files, and auto-save files.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Lem persistence defaults have not been audited against this policy.

target_semantics: Toggle visual line wrapping without changing the underlying buffer.
divergence: The binding uses Lem line wrapping; visual-line navigation parity is unverified.

target_semantics: Expose interactive multiple-cursor editing only if the Emacs configuration actually does so.
divergence: Emacs only uses multiple-cursors internally for a streaming cursor overlay.
notes: No interactive port target is implied by package declaration alone.

target_semantics: Show command candidates automatically; support Prescient literal, regexp, and initialism matching; cycle and persist learned ranking without closing on Space.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim is scoped to the prompt behaviors exercised by the real TUI suite.

target_semantics: Apply Prescient filtering and learned ranking to buffer-switching prompts while retaining filename annotations.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Wiring exists, but there is no TUI assertion for buffer candidates or annotations.

target_semantics: Keep file completion on partial path-component matching while applying learned candidate ranking.
divergence: unassessed
notes: The wrapper preserves the stock path provider and adds ranking; equivalence is not yet TUI-tested.

target_semantics: Annotate completion candidates with useful contextual metadata.
divergence: M-x preserves keybinding annotations, but broader Marginalia category annotations are absent.

target_semantics: Offer automatic in-buffer completion after a 3-character prefix and 0.2-second idle delay in terminal buffers.
divergence: none
notes: The real TUI verifies the threshold, wall-clock delay, debounce, ten-row popup, non-cycling boundaries, singleton display, full-token replacement, movement and buffer-switch cleanup, Escape cancellation, and stale asynchronous rejection.

target_semantics: Compose Cape file and dynamic-abbreviation fallbacks with mode-local completion providers.
divergence: none
notes: Mode-local completion is authoritative, matching live Eglot exclusivity; otherwise same-major-mode dabbrev precedes file-at-point completion, and both fallbacks are exercised through ncurses.

target_semantics: Expand private and community snippets globally with placeholder and field navigation through Tab.
divergence: No snippet parser, placeholder session, mirrored fields, or imported snippet collection is configured.

target_semantics: Find project files, grep a project, switch projects, and switch among project buffers.
divergence: Core commands exist, but Consult live preview and identical candidate metadata are absent.

target_semantics: Search LSP symbols across the workspace from SPC p s.
divergence: The binding invokes document-symbol search rather than workspace-symbol search.

target_semantics: Offer context-sensitive actions for the current candidate or object and integrate those actions with search results.
divergence: No action-at-point framework is configured.

target_semantics: Edit grep result matches in place and write changes back to source files.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Capability survey reports editable grep, but this repository has no behavioral verification.

target_semantics: Keep plain LSP completion display, filtering, and insertion-text precedence distinct; run final acceptance once and reject out-of-order asynchronous results.
divergence: none
notes: The exact claim covers label, filterText, insertText, TextEdit and InsertReplaceEdit newText precedence, focus and acceptance callbacks, singleton acceptance, per-generation origin validation, and logical cancellation; tracked LSP replacement ranges, snippets, and post-accept edits or commands remain separate gaps.

target_semantics: Apply case-smart, space-separated Orderless matching to in-buffer candidates and expose the Corfu separator workflow.
divergence: Lem providers still use their native prefix or fuzzy filtering, and the ordinary-buffer popup has no M-Space separator workflow.
notes: Prompt completion correctly uses Prescient instead and is tracked separately by COMP-001.

target_semantics: Start an LSP client for supported local programming buffers while avoiding inappropriate transient or remote buffers.
divergence: Lem language specs exist, but Emacs hook guards and diagnostics lifecycle policy are not reproduced.

target_semantics: Use rust-analyzer for Rust buffers rooted by Cargo metadata.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Boot checks registration, not a live server handshake.

target_semantics: Use pyright-langserver over stdio for Python projects.
divergence: unassessed
notes: No live Python LSP fixture is checked.

target_semantics: Use harper-ls over stdio for Markdown prose diagnostics.
divergence: unassessed
notes: No live Markdown LSP fixture is checked.

target_semantics: Use nixd with flake-aware nixpkgs and option expressions plus an available Nix formatter.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Initialization options are implemented but not asserted against a live nixd process.

target_semantics: Use gopls for Go buffers and terraform-ls for Terraform buffers.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Relies on upstream Lem specs and has no repository-level handshake test.

target_semantics: Use Eclipse JDT language services and Google Java formatting for Java buffers.
divergence: No jdtls spec or launcher integration is configured.
notes: Syntax highlighting alone is not IDE parity.

target_semantics: Connect GDScript buffers to the Godot language server.
divergence: Lem has no configured GDScript mode or Godot LSP transport.

target_semantics: Provide C-sharp language mode and LSP diagnostics using an available server.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Inventory itself does not pin the Emacs server, and Lem runtime coverage is not audited.

target_semantics: Format the active programming buffer with the language-appropriate formatter.
divergence: Formatting is manual and LSP-only rather than Apheleia format-on-save with CLI fallbacks.

target_semantics: Use LSP diagnostics when managed and a non-LSP linter otherwise.
divergence: LSP diagnostics are available, but there is no Flycheck-equivalent non-LSP linter policy.

target_semantics: Automatically select installed tree-sitter grammars and use high-detail syntax highlighting.
divergence: Tree-sitter support is available but manually enabled; modes default to TextMate highlighting.

target_semantics: Debug Python, Go, Rust, C, and C++ through DAP adapters with breakpoints.
divergence: Lem has no configured DAP client.

target_semantics: Provide Lisp evaluation and an interactive development environment for Common Lisp and Clojure.
divergence: Lem has strong native Lisp tooling, but exact IELM, CIDER, Scheme, and Racket workflows are not equivalent.

target_semantics: Recognize Just, Meson, nginx, Nushell, and Typst files with dedicated major modes.
divergence: These files fall back to fundamental mode.

target_semantics: Open a full Git porcelain supporting status, stage, commit, branch, push, pull, stash, and rebase.
divergence: Legit covers the core porcelain but is not a behavior-identical Magit interface.

target_semantics: Dispatch SPC g g to Jujutsu when inside a jj worktree and otherwise to Git status.
divergence: The jj side is a read-only status and log buffer rather than Majutsu porcelain.

target_semantics: Show per-line Git changes in programming buffers.
divergence: Lem enables git-gutter globally rather than only in programming buffers; visual equivalence is untested.

target_semantics: Step backward and forward through revisions of the current file and jump to a chosen revision.
divergence: Core navigation exists, but no TUI evidence compares its interaction with git-timemachine.

target_semantics: Browse and mutate Jujutsu history through a Magit-style porcelain.
divergence: Only read-only jj status and log output is implemented.
notes: VCS-002 tracks the narrower smart-dispatch approximation.

target_semantics: Display TODO and FIXME items inside the Git status UI.
divergence: Legit has no configured TODO section.

target_semantics: Manage forge pull requests and issues from the editor's Git UI.
divergence: No forge integration is configured.

target_semantics: Start with the editor's default theme unless the host-gated business profile applies.
divergence: Lem and Emacs default themes differ even though neither port explicitly loads a theme.
notes: The absence of a Doom theme is intentional.

target_semantics: Show relative line numbers in programming buffers only.
divergence: Relative line numbers are enabled globally rather than only on programming buffers.

target_semantics: Recenter and briefly pulse the destination after Consult or imenu jumps.
divergence: Lem recenters some jumps but does not reproduce the configured pulse effect and hooks.

target_semantics: Draw indentation guides in programming buffers.
divergence: No indent-bars equivalent is configured for the ncurses frontend.

target_semantics: Color nested delimiters distinctly in programming buffers and highlight matching pairs.
divergence: Paren coloring is Lisp-specific; other programming modes only have matching-pair highlighting.

target_semantics: Select JetBrainsMono Nerd Font with configured fallbacks and size.
divergence: The ncurses frontend inherits font selection from the terminal rather than controlling it.
notes: A graphical Lem frontend would need a separate target row.

target_semantics: Show editor tabs and support undoing and redoing window layouts.
divergence: Frame tabs are enabled, but winner-style layout history is absent.

target_semantics: Replace the stock directory browser with a richer file-management interface.
divergence: Lem provides directory and filer modes but not Dirvish's exact presentation and integrations.

target_semantics: Center document text to a configurable width within the current window.
divergence: No centered-view command or binding is configured.

target_semantics: Apply the office-document visual profile only on configured business hosts and document modes.
divergence: The host-gated business visual and document modes are not ported.

target_semantics: Show available continuations after a prefix and provide transient command menus.
divergence: The Space leader waits silently; upstream transient primitives are not wired into a which-key-style continuation popup.

target_semantics: Resolve the notes root from WORKDIR with a home/work fallback and operate on the same shared files.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Path resolution is implemented but lacks an isolated filesystem test.

target_semantics: Capture inbox, TODO, reading, and public TODO entries into their configured Org locations with CREATED metadata and public IDs.
divergence: File placement and subtree safety match, but the full org-capture interaction, context insertion, and template engine are absent.
notes: The hermetic suite verifies exact Inbox placement, the public target, UUID shape, and timestamps.

target_semantics: Find, insert, and open random Org or Markdown roam notes under WORKDIR/roam.
divergence: File scanning works without org-roam's database, backlinks, aliases, tags, or completion metadata.

target_semantics: Treat Markdown front-matter notes as first-class members of the roam graph.
divergence: Markdown files are discoverable, but there is no graph database or front-matter-aware indexing.

target_semantics: Open today's daily note or a validated date directly under WORKDIR/roam using the established filename layout.
divergence: The safe file layout is preserved, but org-roam capture templates and metadata are reduced.
notes: The suite verifies leap dates, rejects traversal and impossible dates, and forbids the legacy daily subdirectory.

target_semantics: Create journal entries under roam/journal with the configured date filename and heading formats.
divergence: Core file placement exists without org-journal navigation and calendar integrations.

target_semantics: Create or reuse a valid Org ID property on the current heading with SPC m I.
divergence: none
notes: Verified against a plain Org fixture and UUID property drawer.

target_semantics: Display actionable items grouped into useful agenda views from files under WORKDIR.
divergence: The scanner provides overdue, today, upcoming, and TODO groups without org-agenda editing semantics or arbitrary agenda commands.

target_semantics: Edit Org documents with folding, links, tables, TODO workflows, visual styling, and agenda integration.
divergence: Org files open as plain text and Lem has no Org major mode.
notes: File-writing workflows do not imply document-editing parity.

target_semantics: Execute configured Babel languages, apply trust policy, preview LaTeX, and publish notes to HTML.
divergence: No Babel execution or Org publishing layer is configured.

target_semantics: Search bibliography entries and open their files, URLs, or notes from the configured bibliography paths.
divergence: Citar-like lookup exists, but Ebib, RefTeX, org-ref, and richer citation insertion are absent.

target_semantics: Sync selected actionable Org headings to the external nodes graph only on configured hosts.
divergence: The host-gated save hook and graph projection are not configured.
notes: Do not broaden this into automatic IDs for all agenda entries.

target_semantics: Search and read indexed mail, refresh the index, and open relevant message content.
divergence: The CLI-backed reader omits composition, SMTP, attachment PDF preview, and Outlook-specific integration.

target_semantics: Read Miniflux feeds through the Fever API and archive entries through archive.is.
divergence: Core feed listing and reading are ported without Elfeed's full filtering and local database behavior.

target_semantics: Render PDFs inside the editor and preview mail PDF attachments.
divergence: PDFs are opened externally rather than rendered in Lem.

target_semantics: Open and navigate EPUB books inside the editor.
divergence: No EPUB major mode or renderer is configured.

target_semantics: Run an interactive terminal inside the editor for tools such as Claude Code.
divergence: Lem terminal is analogous to vterm, but the configured Claude backend interaction is not verified.

target_semantics: Install DevDocs indexes, search entries, render documentation text, and open the source page in a browser.
divergence: Feature is implemented from the DevDocs API but has no network-isolated behavior test.

target_semantics: Connect to PostgreSQL, list tables, run queries, render result tables, and refresh the last query.
divergence: The psql-backed viewer is narrower than pgmacs and lacks editing and object-browser depth.

target_semantics: Search properties and inspect property, contractor, reckoner, financial, and payment data through the Salta prefix.
divergence: The major workflows are implemented, but live API and payment-email bridge parity are unverified.

target_semantics: Stream prompts to OpenRouter using openrouter/auto, a short system message, and an environment-provided API key.
divergence: Streaming exists without gptel's conversation structure, model discovery cache, role UI, or stability layer.

target_semantics: Use GitHub Copilot and Perplexity backends when credentials are available.
divergence: Only OpenRouter and CLI backends are configured.

target_semantics: Load and save named LLM presets and hand the current context to external Claude or ChatGPT applications.
divergence: Model prompting and backend selection exist, but saved presets and external-app handoff URLs are absent.

target_semantics: Drive Claude Code, Codex, and Grok CLI backends with streaming output and resumable sessions.
divergence: CLI adapters exist, but session metadata, agent rendering, and every gptel backend option are not equivalent.

target_semantics: Authenticate ChatGPT Codex and Grok Build through OAuth and stream their native HTTP backends.
divergence: OAuth and token-refresh flows are not implemented.

target_semantics: Open an IDE-integrated Claude Code session from normal state.
divergence: unassessed
notes: The binding exists, but no controlled Claude session test verifies terminal and diff behavior.

target_semantics: Allow Claude Code to drive editor buffers and present changes through an MCP or websocket bridge with diff review.
divergence: Lem exposes related server capabilities, but Monet's websocket and ediff workflow is not reproduced exactly.

target_semantics: Act as a generic MCP client hub that starts configured fetch and GitHub servers and exposes their tools to the LLM.
divergence: Lem's configured capability is primarily an MCP server, not a generic client hub.

target_semantics: Expose read-only project, file-search, file-read, and symbol-reading tools to agentic LLM presets.
divergence: The OpenRouter client does not implement gptel's tool loop.

target_semantics: Relocate package state away from configuration directories before packages initialize.
divergence: Lem and ASDF use different state locations; the Nix wrapper already directs build outputs to XDG cache.
notes: This is platform plumbing rather than user-visible parity.

target_semantics: Tune garbage collection thresholds after startup to reduce Emacs pauses.
divergence: Emacs gcmh tuning does not map directly to SBCL's collector.
notes: Runtime performance should be measured independently.

target_semantics: Load the current direnv environment when entering a project.
divergence: No direnv integration is configured.

target_semantics: Transparently decrypt and re-encrypt SOPS-managed files while editing.
divergence: No global SOPS file handler is configured.

target_semantics: Edit grep matches and persist the changes back to their source files.
divergence: unassessed
notes: The capability survey reports this behavior but the local test gate does not exercise it.

target_semantics: Describe commands, variables, keys, and bindings from the leader help prefix.
divergence: Lem core help is used rather than Helpful's richer source and cross-reference presentation.

target_semantics: Honor EditorConfig rules in programming buffers.
divergence: Only the fixed tab width is configured; project EditorConfig files are not applied.

target_semantics: Start quietly and quickly without toolbars, menus, startup text, or configuration compilation noise.
divergence: The wrapper starts quietly and caches ASDF outputs, but Emacs early-init tuning has no exact Lem analogue.

target_semantics: Start a reusable editor server and point EDITOR, VISUAL, and GIT_EDITOR at its client.
divergence: The configured wrapper starts a standalone ncurses editor process, not an Emacsclient-equivalent daemon workflow.

target_semantics: Use ripgrep for project search with useful defaults and ignored build or VCS directories.
divergence: The command and basic flags are set, but the Emacs ignored-directory policy is not fully mirrored.

target_semantics: Automatically revert changed files and persist recent files, places, prompt history, kill ring, registers, marks, and searches.
divergence: Some upstream histories may persist, but the configured Emacs auto-revert and expanded savehist set are not implemented.
notes: A future assessment may split this broad row.

target_semantics: Compile configuration ahead of time and avoid writing compiled artifacts into the source tree.
divergence: The Nix wrapper redirects ASDF outputs to cache and loads on demand rather than AOT native-compiling every source.

target_semantics: Treat daily modal editing, leader bindings, completion, navigation, editing defaults, structural editing, and UI basics as the first acceptance tier.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: The behavior rows above carry the actionable dispositions and evidence.

target_semantics: Treat language tooling, VCS, notes, debugging, and AI entry points as the second acceptance tier.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: Rows may use P1 or P2 based on current daily impact while preserving the inventory tier as source context.

target_semantics: Track bespoke applications and host-gated integrations explicitly even where Lem has no counterpart.
divergence: Priority classification is planning metadata rather than runtime behavior.
notes: Gaps remain visible instead of disappearing from package-centric summaries.

target_semantics: Do not port a Doom modeline that is referenced only by a dead customization variable and is not installed.
divergence: The reference has no active target semantics.
notes: Keep dead configuration out of implementation scope.

target_semantics: Assess default-only language packages before claiming their modes or workflows are required.
divergence: unassessed
notes: Explicitly separates package installation from configured behavior.

target_semantics: Assess default-only Org and publishing packages before adding new port scope.
divergence: unassessed
notes: No behavior should be inferred solely from package presence.

target_semantics: Assess nov and pgmacs defaults independently from the explicit app workflows already tracked.
divergence: unassessed
